details: The DOI is present in the paper, and the experimental section quote supports cyanohydrin 2 with mp 119-121 °C and measured data type.
The source characterization for compound 26b reads (2R,4R,E), while the row and claimed quote identify (2S,4S,E); the claimed quote is not present as stated.
The PMC source and spectral data section support Philinopgenin A with mp 208.5-208.5 °C as a measured melting point.

The PDF text supports compound 2 and mp 285-286 °C, but the asserted quote is not present under whitespace and hyphen normalization because the source text line-breaks/hyphenates the compound name differently.
The DOI is present, and the characterization section supports 4-Benzhydryl-N-benzylpiperazine-1-carbothioamide (3n) as a white solid with 156.54 °C in the melting-point context.
The specified paper_path is an HTML Europe PMC shell with no article text; the asserted Ag(AN)(OTf) quote and 148 °C value are not present in that source file.
The DOI is present, and the characterization text supports compound 6b with m.p. 255-259 °C as a measured melting point.
The DOI is present, and the spectral data section supports Soulattrin (1) with m.p. 180-181 °C as measured.
The DOI is present, and the characterization text supports compound 4E with mp 115 °C as a measured melting point.
The DOI is present, Table 5 binds Thalidomide to the literature Tm value 543.15 K, and the conversion to 270.00 °C is correct.
The source quote prints the melting range as mp: 220-22°C for compound 3l, but the row's value_raw is expanded to 220-222 °C rather than matching the printed raw value.
The DOI is present, and Table 1 compound II binds bis(3-nitrobenzylidene)-1,3-diaminopropane to M.P. 112-114 °C with measured data type.
The source quote prints the melting range as mp: 154- 55°C for compound 7c, but the row's value_raw is expanded to 154-155 °C rather than matching the printed raw value.
The DOI is present, and the section for compound 6b supports mp 232-234 °C as a measured melting point.
The DOI is present, and the HTML visible text supports compound 7 with Mp: 105-106 o C as measured.
The DOI is present, and the spectral data section supports Caledonixanthone G (8) with melting point 201°C as measured.
The DOI is present, and the characterization text supports compound 4h with mp = 180-182 °C as measured.
The DOI is present, and the characterization section supports N,4-Diphenylpiperazine-1-carbothioamide (3a) with 160.38 °C in the melting-point context.
The DOI is present, and the experimental section binds compound 4c to the silyl ester MPA analogue context with Mp: 48-50 °C as measured.
The DOI is present, and the characterization text supports Propyle 1-hydroxy-1H-indole-2-carboxylate 2c with mp 170-172 °C as measured.

The source section identifies the compound as 1-(2,4-Difluorophenyl)-2-(1H-1,2,4-triazol-1-yl)ethanol (10), while the row uses the generic name fluorinated nitroazole derivative and the quote lacks the row compound name.
The DOI is present, Table 5 binds Camostat to the literature Tm value 467.15 K, and the conversion to 194.00 °C is correct.
The DOI is present, and the synthesis section supports compound AL5 with M.p. 275.5 °C (dec.) as measured decomposition/melting behavior.
The DOI is present, and the section for compound 12a supports mp 244-246 °C as a measured melting point.
The DOI is present, and the characterization section supports compound 6b with M.P. 118-120 °C as measured.
